name: Esports Matchday Command
description: A full-bleed cinematic esports homepage pattern built around a live match hero, broadcast telemetry, roster matchup panel, vertical update reel, and aggressive gaming CTAs.

broadcast-home.description: Full homepage treatment with fixed immersive header, first-viewport match stage, right-side 5v5 roster panel, vertical live update reel, command layers section, and access teaser.

hero-only.description: First-viewport match hero only. Use when the page needs a cinematic top section but the rest of the route is composed from separate product patterns.

compact.description: Compressed broadcast stage for route previews and smaller public pages. Keeps headline, live score, CTAs, and roster proof while dropping secondary command-layer sections.

visual_brief: The page should feel like a professional live esports broadcast command surface, not a generic SaaS landing page. The first viewport is a dark arena-like stage with cyan, acid green, violet, and red light beams over a subtle perspective grid. A huge italic uppercase match headline names a specific live match, for example 'Team Vitality vs. r3Fraction'. The headline stays white while a narrow gleam sweeps left-to-right on load and then repeats every few seconds. Supporting copy is short and operational. A compact telemetry strip sits beneath it with current score, map, map-ban timer, comms, form, and momentum. Primary actions use angular cut/parallelogram buttons, not pills. The right third of desktop contains a liquid-glass roster panel with a score row at the top, two-column 5v5 circular-avatar player matchups, and a vertical rolling update reel in the empty lower space. The bottom page ticker is forbidden; live updates belong inside the roster panel. Ambient animation may include a radar/galaxy layer on desktop top-left and mobile right-side, but avoid mascot, controller, or decorative character graphics that compete with the copy.

layout_hints.container: full-bleed route section, never a d-surface card
layout_hints.shell: Prefer immersive-stage. If top-nav-footer is used, override its default section padding and make the header flush full-width.
layout_hints.headline: Use bold italic display typography with no negative letter spacing. Text should remain white at rest; shimmer is an overlay only.
layout_hints.buttons: Use angular clipped buttons with hover gleam, slight skew/translate on hover, and active press feedback. Avoid rounded pill CTAs.
layout_hints.roster_panel: Desktop panel is about one third of viewport width. It starts with the score row, not an extra 'Live roster / 5v5' label strip.
layout_hints.updates: Use a vertical rolling update reel inside the roster panel. Duplicate reel items for seamless looping. Fade top/bottom edges.
layout_hints.anti_patterns: Do not use generic hero paragraph CTA card scaffolds, chip-heavy hero badges, nested cards, bottom horizontal marquees, mascot/controller hero graphics, or blocky grid-card wrapping in the first viewport.

slots.broadcast-stage: Full-viewport background stage with perspective grid, scanline/grain texture, and colored light beams.
slots.match-headline: Large match title with explicit teams and small VS tag tucked into the line flow.
slots.live-telemetry-strip: Inline score and operating metrics below supporting copy.
slots.cut-button: Primary and secondary angular CTAs with icon support and animated hover state.
slots.live-roster-panel: Glass panel containing score row, 5v5 player avatar matchups, and update reel.
slots.vertical-update-reel: Top-to-bottom rolling match updates contained inside the roster panel.
slots.command-layer-runway: Second section with three free-form operating layers for pressure, replay, and partner control.
slots.access-teaser: Login or access teaser can use a split scene later on the page, not inside the first hero card.

composition.EsportsMatchdayCommand: Page(full-bleed) > BroadcastStage > [AmbientCanvas + HeaderShell + HeroGrid > [HeroCopy > [Eyebrow + MatchHeadline + Lede + LiveTelemetryStrip + ActionDock] + LiveRosterPanel > [ScoreRow + RosterGrid + VerticalUpdateReel]]] + CommandLayerRunway + AccessTeaser
composition.MatchHeadline: Heading(display, italic, uppercase) > [TeamName(primary) + VsTag + TeamName(opponent)] with TitleGleam overlay
composition.LiveTelemetryStrip: Masked horizontal grid > [ScoreCell + TimerCell + CommsCell + FormCell + MomentumCell]
composition.LiveRosterPanel: GlassPanel(liquid, translucent) > [ScoreRow + RosterGrid(5 rows, 2 columns) + VerticalUpdateReel]
composition.VerticalUpdateReel: MaskedViewport(height fixed) > ReelTrack(duplicated updates, translateY loop) > UpdateRow[] > [Timestamp + EventText]
composition.CommandLayerRunway: Open full-width section with three offset layers, each border-accented by a different match color

keyboard: Tab: move through navigation, CTAs, roster panel, and access form
Space: pause live update reel when focused if implemented as an interactive control
accessibility.focus_management: Animated updates should not steal focus. Provide readable text alternatives for canvas-only ambience.

responsive.mobile: Single-column stage. Header hides secondary nav links. Headline reduces enough to fit without clipping. Telemetry becomes two columns, then one column below very narrow widths. Roster panel stacks beneath CTAs; player matchups become vertical pairs. Radar ambience may move to the right side and should never obscure the lede.
responsive.tablet: Hero copy stays first, roster panel moves beneath or beside depending on available width. Keep the match title readable and preserve the score row.
responsive.desktop: Hero uses two columns: copy consumes remaining width, roster panel occupies roughly one third. Ambient radar sits top-left. Header accent line spans full viewport width.

ambient.title-gleam: Overlay shimmer sweeps left-to-right on load and repeats about every 4 seconds while base text stays white.
ambient.radar: Subtle rotating radar or orbital particle layer, desktop top-left and mobile right-side.
ambient.vertical-reel: Live update reel rolls vertically with top and bottom mask fades.
ambient.scanline: Optional low-opacity scanline texture, disabled under reduced motion.

micro.button-hover: Cut buttons translate up, skew slightly, and sweep a light band across the fill.
micro.button-active: Button presses down by 1px and scales slightly.
micro.live-beacon: Primary live CTA includes pulsing dot/ring icon.

transitions.hero-enter: Headline and telemetry fade up quickly; roster panel follows with a soft lift.
